mechanism_narrative: Elevated CPI inflation reduces real corporate earnings expectations and raises the probability of restrictive monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when CPIAUCSL is above its 12-month trailing average over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor demand and falling household income, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In walk-forward tests, triggered instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time, or the mean return during triggers is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and increases in unemployment indicate a coordinated contraction in consumer demand and labor market income, jointly producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days that is stronger than either signal in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS changes and UNRATE changes yields a rank correlation with 60-day forward returns below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS signal in out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% during an active tightening cycle increases discount rates and debt service costs for corporations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the cumulative return during triggered periods is not negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which lifts corporate revenue and profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1400 thousand units signals a contraction in residential construction and associated durable goods demand, reducing aggregate demand expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where HOUST is below 1400 fail to produce negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and a high federal funds rate raises real interest rates and borrowing costs simultaneously, creating a joint drag on equity valuations that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A linear model including the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction term yields a t-statistic below 1.5 for the interaction coefficient over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction strategy underperforms a simple FEDFUNDS-only strategy in risk-adjusted returns.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth exceeding 2.0% over three months signals an overheating economy that invites monetary policy restraint, leading markets to price in future tightening and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of INDPRO 3-month changes above 2.0% produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or the average forward return during triggers is not negative over a 3-year rolling window.
